rules: RULE-01: input has level_id or an inline level (grid-row strings). '#' wall, ' ' floor, '.' target, '@' player, '$' box, '*' box-on-target, '+' player-on-target; any other char is a generic tile in state.tiles. Apply input.moves in order up to MAX_MOVES.
RULE-02: A move steps the player one cell; into floor/target advances; log '<dir>:move'.
RULE-03: Into a wall is blocked; log '<dir>:block'.
RULE-04: Into a box: push if the cell beyond is free; log '<dir>:push'; else blocked.
RULE-05: WIN when every box is on a target; set isWon=isGameOver=true and stop. on_target = boxes on targets.
RULE-06: KEY/DOOR (card 1). 'D' blocks while no key; 'K' grants key (+key, K removed); with key 'D' is floor.
RULE-07: TELEPORT (card 2). Player stepping onto 'T' jumps to the matching other 'T' (+tp), once per move; boxes never teleport.

RULE-08 (THIS CARD): HOLE tiles. The level data (including a level with 'O' tiles) is ALREADY in the frozen levels.js — this card edits ONLY move_logic.js. When a box is pushed into a cell that is a hole 'O', the hole is filled: remove that box from state.boxes, delete the 'O' from state.tiles (it becomes floor), advance the player into the box's old cell, and log '<dir>:fill' (instead of ':push'). A filled box no longer counts toward the win. Any play on a level with no 'O' tiles produces byte-for-byte identical output to before this card.

files.description: Move resolution. THIS card adds the hole case inside the push branch: if the box's destination cell is an 'O' tile, fill it (remove box, delete tile, move player, log ':fill'). Keep all existing wall/push/move/door/key/teleport behavior intact. levels.js is FROZEN.
